prevalence.value: 15-25% (adults; higher in urban and older populations)
prevalence.trend: increasing with screen time, pollution, and aging

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Symptoms plus clinical signs; objective testing in eye clinics; rule out blepharitis and infection

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on access to lubricants and eye care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often self-managed; clinical diagnosis in eye clinics and some primary care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lubricants, lid hygiene, environmental modifications; topical anti-inflammatories in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High pollution exposure and out-of-pocket cost for long-term lubricants
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large eye care networks and low-risk symptomatic treatments
